title: Hi, I'm Karthik - Cloud Security Engineer
excerpt: Cloud Security Engineer obsessed with AWS, IAM, and making sure the things we build don't end up in a breach report. This is my corner of the internet.
content:
# Hi, I'm Karthik

Cloud Security Engineer. I break things in AWS so others don't have to learn the hard way.

This is my corner of the internet — a place where I write about what I'm learning, building, and occasionally staying up until 2 AM debugging.

---

## What I Do

I work in cloud security, primarily on AWS infrastructure. My day-to-day involves:

- **IAM & Access Control** — designing least-privilege policies, trust boundaries, and making sure no one has `*:*` anywhere near production
- **Threat Modeling** — mapping out attack surfaces before someone else does
- **Security Automation** — building guardrails so humans aren't the last line of defense
- **Incident Response** — investigating what went wrong and making sure it can't happen again

If it lives in the cloud and can go wrong, I'm probably thinking about it.

---

## Background

I got into cloud security because the cloud moves fast and security often doesn't. That gap is where most breaches happen — not from sophisticated zero-days, but from misconfigurations, overpermissioned roles, and defaults no one questioned.

I find that interesting. And fixable.

Before focusing on security, I worked across cloud infrastructure and backend systems, which gives me a builder's perspective on security problems. I understand why engineers make the shortcuts they make — and how to design security that doesn't slow them down.

---

## What I'm Building

Right now I'm focused on:

- Deepening expertise in AWS security services — GuardDuty, Security Hub, IAM Access Analyzer
- Writing up the things I learn that I couldn't easily find documented elsewhere
- Contributing to the security community through this blog and [DEV.to](https://dev.to/karthiblip)

---

— Karthik
